name: License-Aware Slurm Job
summary: Plan Slurm jobs that request and verify tracked software license resources.
description: This skill helps users and support teams make licensed software jobs explicit by checking visible Slurm license resources, documenting optional license-manager evidence, and adding bounded Slurm --licenses requests before running commercial or limited-seat applications.

tools.name: lmutil
tools.notes: Optional external license-manager evidence when the site permits users to query it.

inputs.name: account
inputs.description: Site-specific Slurm account or allocation for the licensed software test job.

inputs.name: partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue for the licensed software test job.

inputs.name: license_resource
inputs.description: Public-safe Slurm license resource name or placeholder, such as app-feature.

inputs.name: license_count
inputs.description: Number of license tokens or seats requested by the small test job.

inputs.name: application_command
inputs.description: Small licensed application command to run only after dry-run review.

inputs.name: optional_license_status_command
inputs.description: Optional site-approved read-only command for external license-manager status checks.

outputs.name: license_status_report
outputs.description: Read-only evidence from visible Slurm license resources and optional site-approved license-manager checks.

outputs.name: submission_plan
outputs.description: Dry-run sbatch command showing account, partition, --licenses, exported app settings, and template path.

outputs.name: slurm_job_id
outputs.description: Job id printed by sbatch only when SUBMIT_LICENSED_JOB=1 is explicitly set.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check shell syntax for the planner and Slurm template.

Run the planner with SUBMIT_LICENSED_JOB=0 to review visible license evidence and the sbatch command, then submit a tiny test with SUBMIT_LICENSED_JOB=1 and RUN_LICENSED_APP=0 before enabling the licensed command.
